svg.text: Hebrew Handwriting Crowdsource Platform · FastAPI · PostgreSQL
Submit text | cant_read | bad_crop | not_hebrew | not_text
1. Extract JWT from Authorization: Bearer header
2. PROD: verify Google OIDC token → decode claims
3. DEV: skip verify → return dev@localhost user
Consent version enforces re-consent on license updates
Raises HTTP 401 if missing in prod
1. Find page w/ most eligible lines for user
2. Load all lines on chosen page
4. Increment count only on first response
DATABASE · PostgreSQL 15+ · SQLAlchemy 2.0 ORM · Alembic Migrations
Count increments once per user (not on edit)
Line crops rendered via CSS bbox clip
Google OIDC subject as identity key
UNIQUE(line_id, user_id): one row per (line, user)
UPSERT on edit → update in-place (no dup rows)
Count ++ only on INSERT, never on UPDATE
Flags = text | cant_read | bad_crop | not_hebrew | not_text
1. Frontend calls Google Sign-In → receives ID Token (JWT)
2. Frontend sends token via Authorization: Bearer <id_token>
3. Backend verifies token signature + expiry + audience
4. Extracts sub (stable user ID) + email + name claims
google_sub is the stable identity anchor across email changes
⚠ Production OIDC verification stub — needs implementation
▸ PROD: s3://bucket/{image_path} OR CDN URL (one-line swap)
Images stored once per page — lines are CSS-cropped views
bbox {x, y, w, h} drives clip on client (no per-line image fetches)
image_path column on pages table is the storage pointer
Function signature stable: swap backend without touching business logic
Surya OCR segmentation JSON → import pipeline → DB
Idempotency guarantees — re-run same import = no side effects
Surya fields preserved: bbox, polygon, line_index, detection_confidence
Batch source + license flow down hierarchy: batch → page → line
detect_lines.py: wraps Surya, writes segmentation JSON → DB import script
Provenance immutable after import — source/license never reconstructed
Image files placed in storage; image_path stored on pages row
Alembic manages schema migrations — versioned, repeatable
Pillow + numpy available for future image pre-processing steps
Surya OCR: open-source Hebrew-capable line detector (external dep)
Non-Goals: no consensus, no majority vote, no normalization in backend
Consumer decides interpretation: majority text, flag ratio, quality filter
Anonymization (if any) done in export step, not in collection
Provenance exported verbatim: source, license, Surya metadata
All transcriptions per line exported (text + all flag kinds)
Dataset usable for training Hebrew OCR/HTR models
Export script planned — not yet implemented (Slice 10 backlog)
count increments only on first resp
▸ Append-truth: one row per (line, user) — edits are in-place UPSERTs
▸ Cached counter: transcription_count increments only on first response
▸ Over-collection accepted: 4th+ response succeeds; dispatch filters, not DB
▸ Distinct-user: a line never re-served to the same user
▸ Flags = transcriptions: all 5 kinds count toward 3-response target
▸ Provenance immutable: source/license set at import, never changed
▸ No interpretation: backend collects faithfully; consensus done offline
▸ Pure rules: eligibility/counting logic in plain functions (no DB)
▸ Thin endpoints: zero business logic in route handlers
